active_tasks_found: MARKET_COOLDOWN_PERSIST_001 (ACTIVE — task JSON confirmed, prerequisite BRIDGE_ENTRY_GATE_DUPE_SLUG_FIX_001 DONE)

exact_sections_changed.old:
"exit_reason_counts": dict(_exit_reason_counts),
"market_cooldowns_active": sum(
    1 for exp in _market_cooldown.values() if time.time() < exp
),
exact_sections_changed.effect: Serializes the full market_cooldown dict (non-expired entries only) into state.json on every _write_state() call. market_cooldowns_active count field preserved unchanged.
On startup, reads state.json and restores any non-expired cooldown expiry timestamps into _market_cooldown. Fails silently if state.json does not exist (first run) or is corrupt.

old_behavior: _market_cooldown was a module-level in-memory dict never written to persistent storage. state.json only recorded market_cooldowns_active (a count). On restart, _market_cooldown was always empty, so all cooldowns were lost and markets could reopen immediately.
new_behavior: Every _write_state() call now writes market_cooldown_expiry (the dict of market_id → expiry_timestamp, non-expired only) into state.json. On startup, main() reads this key and repopulates _market_cooldown with any entries whose expiry is still in the future. Cooldown semantics (per-reason durations: 600s near_resolution, 1800s stop_loss, 3600s gap_stop) are completely unchanged.
cooldown_write_location: bot_core.py close path — lines 643/646/649: _market_cooldown[trade.market_id] = time.time() + <duration>. Now also serialized via _write_state() payload key market_cooldown_expiry.
cooldown_reload_location: bot_core.py main() startup block — reads STATE_PATH, iterates market_cooldown_expiry, restores non-expired entries into _market_cooldown global.

verification.grep_verify: market_cooldown_expiry and 'Restored.*market cooldown' both present and correctly placed in bot_core.py

restart_notes: Yes — restart required for fix to take effect. The new serialization path only writes market_cooldown_expiry on the next _write_state() call. The reload path only runs on startup. Both require a fresh process. Additionally, the stale __pycache__/bot_core.cpython-*.pyc must be deleted before restart per OPERATOR_ACTION_REQUIRED_001.
summary: Added market_cooldown_expiry dict to _write_state() payload and a startup load block in main() so non-expired cooldown timestamps survive restart and are restored into _market_cooldown before the first loop runs.
